 {"id":"bd-751","title":"Implement discriminator for union types","description":"## Description

Support tagged unions with discriminator field.

## Python Behavior

```python
from typing import Union, Literal

class Cat(SQLModel):
    pet_type: Literal['cat'] = 'cat'
    meows: int

class Dog(SQLModel):
    pet_type: Literal['dog'] = 'dog'
    barks: int

class Pet(SQLModel):
    pet: Union[Cat, Dog] = Field(discriminator='pet_type')
```

## Rust Implementation

Use Rust enums with serde tag:
```rust
#[derive(Model, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(tag = "pet_type")]
enum Pet {
    #[serde(rename = "cat")]
    Cat { meows: i32 },
    #[serde(rename = "dog")]
    Dog { barks: i32 },
}
```

Or with structs:
```rust
#[derive(Model)]
struct Owner {
    #[sqlmodel(discriminator = "pet_type")]
    pet: PetUnion,
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] discriminator attribute parsed
- [ ] Correct serde tag generated
- [ ] Works with nested unions
- [ ] Validation of discriminator values

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (crates/sqlmodel-macros/src/discriminator.rs)
- [ ] Test discriminator field parsing
- [ ] Test union type resolution
- [ ] Test discriminator in validation
- [ ] Test discriminator in serialization

### E2E Tests (tests/e2e/discriminator.rs)
- [ ] Union type with discriminator
- [ ] model_validate uses discriminator
- [ ] model_dump includes discriminator
- [ ] Nested unions with discriminator
- [ ] Invalid discriminator value error

### Logging
- [ ] DEBUG: Discriminator resolution
- [ ] TRACE: Union type matching
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T05:03:20.236444032Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T13:02:01.669470402Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T13:02:01.669402816Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-751","depends_on_id":"bd-1cr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:11Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":64,"issue_id":"bd-751","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented discriminator attribute support:

The sqlmodel discriminator provides schema/documentation metadata.","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:01:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-88i","title":"Phase 7: Database Driver Console Integration","description":"## Purpose
Integrate the console output system into each database driver crate, providing rich feedback for connection establishment, query execution, and driver-specific operations.

## Background
Each driver (PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL) has unique operations that benefit from rich output:
- Connection establishment with progress/status
- Authentication feedback (especially multi-step like SCRAM)
- Query timing and explain plan visualization
- Driver-specific features (PostgreSQL COPY, SQLite pragmas, MySQL procedures)

## Key Deliverables

### 1. sqlmodel-postgres Console Integration
- Connection establishment progress (DNS resolve -> TCP connect -> SSL -> Auth)
- SCRAM authentication step visualization
- Query timing display
- EXPLAIN/EXPLAIN ANALYZE visualization with tree rendering
- COPY progress for bulk operations

### 2. sqlmodel-sqlite Console Integration
- Database file operations (open, create, attach)
- PRAGMA visualization
- Transaction state indicator
- WAL checkpoint progress

### 3. sqlmodel-mysql Console Integration
- Connection establishment with capability negotiation
- Authentication plugin feedback
- Query timing
- SHOW commands formatting

## Integration Pattern
Each driver implements ConsoleAware trait:
- fn set_console(&mut self, console: Option<Arc<SqlModelConsole>>)
- Operations check if console is Some and output mode is Rich
- All output goes to stderr (non-semantic)
- Plain mode outputs nothing or minimal text

## Agent Safety
All driver console output:
1. Goes to stderr only (stdout reserved for query results)
2. Can be disabled via output mode
3. Plain mode emits parseable text only

## Dependencies
- Phase 2 (Core Infrastructure) - SqlModelConsole
- Phase 6 (Pool & Operations) - progress components
- Respective driver crates must be ready

## Verification
- Test each driver with console enabled/disabled
- Verify agent mode produces no rich output
- Performance test (console output must not slow queries)","acceptance_criteria":"PostgreSQL driver implements ConsoleAware trait
SQLite driver implements ConsoleAware trait
MySQL driver implements ConsoleAware trait
Connection events emit console output when enabled
Error states display through console
All unit tests pass for driver integration
Integration tests verify end-to-end console output","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:13:08.008521817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:18:13.309232752Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:18:11.773269385Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-88i","depends_on_id":"bd-1q2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-88i","depends_on_id":"bd-eqb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:11Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-88i","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] PostgreSQL driver implements ConsoleAware trait
- [ ] SQLite driver implements ConsoleAware trait
- [ ] MySQL driver implements ConsoleAware trait
- [ ] Connection progress shown in Rich mode
- [ ] Query timing displayed when console attached
- [ ] EXPLAIN visualization for PostgreSQL
- [ ] No output when console not attached
- [ ] All driver integration tests pass","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:37:32Z"},{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-88i","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Phase 7 complete: All three database drivers now have console integration. PostgreSQL (bd-vz2), SQLite (bd-s6v), and MySQL (bd-no6) all implement ConsoleAware trait with query timing, driver-specific output formatting, and comprehensive test coverage.","created_at":"2026-01-21T21:18:13Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-88wb","title":"schema-diff: coalesce SQLite table-recreate ops per table","description":"Current diff/DDL can emit multiple SQLite operations that each require table recreation (DROP COLUMN / ALTER COLUMN / ADD/DROP PK/FK/UNIQUE). Each op carries a TableInfo snapshot which becomes stale after the first recreation. Add an optimizer that merges recreate-requiring ops per table into a single recreate plan, or simulate TableInfo mutations across ops so each op has correct table_info.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:48:57.902062656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:48:57.902062656Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-88wb","title":"schema-diff: coalesce SQLite table-recreate ops per table","description":"Current diff/DDL can emit multiple SQLite operations that each require table recreation (DROP COLUMN / ALTER COLUMN / ADD/DROP PK/FK/UNIQUE). Each op carries a TableInfo snapshot which becomes stale after the first recreation. Add an optimizer that merges recreate-requiring ops per table into a single recreate plan, or simulate TableInfo mutations across ops so each op has correct table_info.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:48:57.902062656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:13:55.276819325Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9a25","title":"EPIC: Value Type Safety — fix silent data corruption in numeric conversions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:58:59.785966115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:25:03.076274727Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:25:02.450653418Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-9a25","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Background

The `Value` enum in sqlmodel-core/src/value.rs has several lossy type conversions that silently corrupt data:

1. **`From<u64> for Value`** (line 254): Casts u64 to i64 via `as i64`. For values > i64::MAX (9,223,372,036,854,775,807), this silently wraps to negative numbers. This is a DATA CORRUPTION bug.

2. **`TryFrom<Value> for f32`** (lines 411-416): Converts f64/i32/i64 to f32 via `as f32`. For i32 > 16,777,216 or f64 values outside f32 range, precision is silently lost.

3. **`TryFrom<Value> for f64`** (line 436): Converts i64 to f64 via `as f64`. For i64 > 2^53, precision is silently lost.

4. **`FromValue for f32`** in row.rs (lines 408-425): Same lossy conversions when reading from database rows.

## Why This Matters

These conversions happen in the core data path — every value read from or written to a database flows through `Value`. Silent data corruption in this layer is catastrophic:
- Financial amounts could be wrong
- IDs could collide
- Timestamps could wrap
- Users would never know their data is corrupted

## Strategy

For u64→i64: Either add a `Value::UBigInt(u64)` variant or change `From<u64>` to `TryFrom<u64>`. The TryFrom approach is less invasive but removes the ergonomic From impl.

For f32/f64 precision: Add range checks that return errors instead of silently truncating.

## Why P1

Data integrity is non-negotiable for a database library. Silent corruption is worse than a crash — at least a crash is noticed.
","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:59:00Z"},{"id":68,"issue_id":"bd-9a25","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"EPIC complete. All 4 child tasks closed:
- bd-1epw: Fixed From<u64> silent overflow with saturating conversion
- bd-31ed: Fixed lossy f32 conversions with precision-loss detection
- bd-21wb: Fixed lossy i64-to-f64 conversion with precision-loss detection
- bd-1bt4: Added comprehensive conversion boundary tests","created_at":"2026-01-30T04:25:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-9b5","title":"Create visual example programs for console features","description":"## Purpose
Create example programs that demonstrate and visually verify all console renderables, serving as both documentation and visual regression tests.

## Background
Visual examples serve multiple purposes:
1. Documentation for users
2. Visual regression testing
3. Development debugging
4. Feature showcasing

## Implementation Details

### Example Programs

#### examples/console_demo.rs
Comprehensive demo of all renderables:
- ErrorPanel with various error types
- QueryResultTable with sample data
- SchemaTree with multi-table schema
- Progress bars at different states
- Themed output variations

#### examples/error_showcase.rs
All error panel variations:
- Connection errors (with context)
- Query errors (with SQL highlight)
- Validation errors (with suggestions)
- Internal errors (with debug info)
- Error chains (nested errors)

#### examples/query_results.rs
Table output variations:
- Small result set (fits terminal)
- Wide result set (needs horizontal scroll)
- Long result set (needs vertical pagination)
- Various data types (numbers, strings, dates, nulls)
- Unicode content handling

#### examples/progress_demo.rs
Progress indicator showcase:
- Determinate progress at various %
- Indeterminate spinners (all styles)
- Batch operation tracker
- Pool status dashboard
- Combined multi-progress display

#### examples/schema_visualization.rs
Schema display variations:
- Single table (TableInfo panel)
- Multi-table schema (SchemaTree)
- Migration status display
- DDL syntax highlighting

### Example Structure
Each example follows pattern:
fn main() {
    let console = SqlModelConsole::new();
    console.rule(Some("Section Title"));
    
    // Demo content
    console.print_renderable(&renderable);
    
    console.rule(None);
    
    // Plain mode comparison
    println!("\n--- Plain Mode ---");
    let plain_console = SqlModelConsole::plain();
    plain_console.print_renderable(&renderable);
}

### Running Examples
cargo run --example console_demo --features console
cargo run --example error_showcase --features console
...

### Screenshot Documentation
Each example should be run and screenshot captured for docs:
- Terminal with dark theme
- Terminal with light theme
- Different terminal widths

## Verification Steps
1. Each example compiles and runs
2. Visual output matches expectations
3. Plain mode output is parseable
4. Examples work with/without TTY
5. Examples serve as regression tests

## Dependencies
- All renderables implemented
- Theme system complete
- Sample data generators","acceptance_criteria":"Examples demonstrate all renderable types
Examples show both Rich and Plain mode output
Examples include error handling scenarios
Examples are runnable with cargo run --example
Examples are documented in README
All examples compile and run correctly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:15:42.806352652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:39:43.177078780Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:39:43.177022313Z","close_reason":"All 5 visual example programs exist and compile successfully: console_demo, error_showcase, query_results, progress_demo, schema_visualization","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9b5","depends_on_id":"bd-18z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-9b5","depends_on_id":"bd-bc1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ahh","title":"Create TableInfo panel for single-table details","description":"## Purpose
Implement a Panel-based renderable that displays comprehensive information about a single database table, including columns, indexes, foreign keys, and statistics.

## Background
When users want to inspect a specific table in detail (similar to psql \d tablename), they need:
- Table name and metadata in a header
- Column details in a formatted table
- Index information
- Foreign key relationships
- Table statistics (row count, size if available)

This is complementary to SchemaTree - SchemaTree shows the overview, TableInfo shows the details.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/table_info.rs

### Key Components
1. TableStats struct - holds optional runtime statistics
2. TableInfo struct - main renderable with schema reference
3. Builder methods for theme, width, stats
4. Internal helpers to build columns table, indexes section, FK section
5. render() method for rich mode using Panel + Table
6. render_plain() method for agent mode

### Features
- Columns displayed in a rich_rust Table within a Panel
- Shows column name, type, nullable, default, constraints
- Indexes section with UNIQUE/INDEX type and columns
- Foreign keys showing local->remote.column relationships
- Optional stats: row count (with thousand separators), size (formatted as KB/MB/GB)

### Helper Functions
- format_number(): Add thousand separators to numbers
- format_bytes(): Convert bytes to human-readable KB/MB/GB

## Usage Example
let table_info = TableInfo::new(&user_table_schema)
    .with_stats(TableStats {
        row_count: Some(10_000),
        size_bytes: Some(2_500_000),
        ..Default::default()
    })
    .width(80);
console.print_renderable(&table_info);

## Verification Steps
1. Test with table having all features (indexes, FKs, defaults)
2. Test with minimal table (just columns)
3. Verify plain text output is parseable
4. Test width constraints
5. Verify stats formatting
6. Test with empty table schema

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-schema for TableSchema types
- rich_rust Panel and Table components
- Theme from this crate","acceptance_criteria":"TableInfo panel shows table name and schema
Panel shows all columns with types
Panel shows primary key information
Panel shows indexes and constraints
Plain mode outputs table info as text
All unit tests verify rendering","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:10:54.832444473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:59:13.582597383Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:59:13.582517104Z","close_reason":"Implemented - parent phase closed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ahh","depends_on_id":"bd-1rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ahh","depends_on_id":"bd-2g8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:11Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":69,"issue_id":"bd-ahh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_table_info_creation - verify construction
2. test_table_info_columns_display - verify column table
3. test_table_info_indexes_section - verify indexes
4. test_table_info_foreign_keys - verify FK display
5. test_table_info_with_stats - verify statistics
6. test_table_info_render_plain - verify text output
7. test_table_info_render_rich - verify panel output
8. test_table_info_width_constraint - verify width
9. test_table_info_empty_table - edge case
10. test_format_number_thousands - verify separators
11. test_format_bytes_units - verify KB/MB/GB","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:17Z"}]}
